weapon_special_rules.Assault: No –1 penalty for moving and shooting. Infantry armed with an Assault weapon also count as Tough Fighters in Close Quarters.
weapon_special_rules.Team: Crew-served weapon: needs two or more soldiers to fire at full effect (e.g. MMG or Bazooka teams).
weapon_special_rules.Fixed: Best fired stationary. Can only target enemies within a 45° front arc; on an Advance it may rotate on the spot but not move, and it can never launch an Assault.
weapon_special_rules.Anti-Tank Gun: +1 to hit Vehicles when shooting with a Fire Order (including Ambush and Reaction Fire).
weapon_special_rules.Anti-tank Launchers: Disposable. One model per Order may fire it in place of its normal weapon; the unit may instead fire them all at once (roll a D6 for shots) and is then out of ammo.
weapon_special_rules.Shaped Charge: Ignores the +1 Pen for point-blank range. Pen is halved (rounding up) against Walkers and Powered Armour.
weapon_special_rules.HE: High Explosive: fires a blast template of the shown diameter and every model touched is hit. HE (X") sets the blast size and Pen. Not modified by range.
weapon_special_rules.Indirect Fire: Lobs shots over intervening troops. Cannot hit targets inside its minimum range; the crew still need line of sight (or a Spotter), and a 6 is needed to range in.
weapon_special_rules.Howitzer: May fire directly with no minimum range, or use Indirect Fire with the minimum range shown in brackets on the chart.
weapon_special_rules.Flamethrower: Ignores Cover saves; a hit becomes D6 hits (D6+1 from a vehicle); always hits Top Armour; inflicts extra Pins and forces a Morale check. May run dry on a roll of 1–2.
weapon_special_rules.Rift Weapon: Has a basic profile that is always usable; exhaust an Active Rift Die to trigger its special Rift ability (a Surging die can Overload it). Pen is not reduced by range.
weapon_special_rules.Tesla Arc: Exhaust a Rift Die to add (X) extra shots (Arcs). While used, the weapon gains +1 to hit Vehicles and ignores some defences.
weapon_special_rules.Corrosive: Exhaust a Rift Die to increase the weapon's Pen against the target by the die type shown (e.g. D2 or D6).
weapon_special_rules.Mode: The weapon has multiple profiles; each model may freely switch profile between shooting and Close Quarters.
weapon_special_rules.Gravity Pulse: A Rift ability: exhaust a Rift Die to unleash a gravity pulse, warping space around the target for extra effect (see Core Rulebook).
weapon_special_rules.Hyper Velocity: Exhaust a Rift Die to fire a Hyper Velocity shot: +1 to hit and +1 Pen, and it ignores Cover saves.
weapon_special_rules.E-Slug: The Thompson M1X1/2's alternate fire profile (it's a Mode weapon). Each time it fires you pick ONE profile: Normal (12", 2 shots, +1, Assault) or E-Slug (6", 1 shot, +2) — a short-range electro-slug that hits vehicle electronics and tough organic targets hard. You don't fire both in one action, and needn't fire Normal first; the 'empty the magazine' line is flavour, not a rule.
